batch_summary: Deed [120] PDF pulled and analyzed — JH Bounds → J.E. Bounds, 158 ac John Boyd League, executed 1874-10-07 (NOT 1877-02-26 per index — that is the filing date). Full fee simple warranty/gift deed, surface+minerals, $1 consideration, no mineral reservation. Metes-and-bounds confirmed: beginning 400 vrs W of SE corner of Boyd League (vs deed [106] at 500 vrs W — different parcel), 500 vrs wide along south line, north to Tehuacana Creek. Eastern boundary follows 'J.V. Bounds west line,' confirming deed [121] (62 ac to J.V. Bounds) was executed contemporaneously ~1874. Date anomaly: acknowledgment (Oct 2, 1877) postdates filing (Feb 26, 1877) and recording (Mar 1, 1877) — unusual sequencing requires human verification. Boyd Portfolio arithmetic updated: total documented dispositions now ~500.8 ac vs 156 ac documented acquisitions, confirming JH Bounds held ≥344.8 ac Boyd Survey land from undocumented pre-1874 sources. ISSUE-40 item [120] marked PULLED. ISSUE-41 partially advanced (metes-and-bounds now available for J.E. Bounds grantor search). DISPOSED OUT OF DIRECT CHAIN — no impact on current Gude/Trust holdings.

02_PROPERTY_INVENTORY.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [120] PDF pulled — Boyd Survey Portfolio updated: [120] (158 ac, 1874) and [121] (62 ac, IDX) added to dispositions table; total documented dispositions now ~500.8 ac; arithmetic gap updated to ~344.8 ac from undocumented pre-1874 sources

operations.description: Update Critical Findings #2 with full disposition arithmetic including [120]/[121] and [318]
operations.find: 2. **JH Bounds' Boyd Survey holdings were substantially larger than the [106] acquisition.** The dispositions in [104] (pre-1886), [108] (156 ac), and [113] (80 ac) total 238 ac, which is 82 ac more than the documented [106] acquisition. This means JH Bounds acquired Boyd Survey land from at least one other source not currently in the file set.
operations.replace_with: 2. **JH Bounds' Boyd Survey holdings were substantially larger than the [106] acquisition.** Total documented dispositions = ~500.8 ac: [120] (158 ac, 1874) + [121] (62 ac, ~1874, IDX) + [104] (2 ac, 1885) + [108] (156 ac, 1889) + [113] (80 ac, 1910) + [318] (~42.8 ac, 1975). This is ~344.8 ac more than the documented [106] acquisition (156 ac). JH Bounds acquired Boyd Survey land from at least one major undocumented source predating 1874. The [120] metes-and-bounds (beginning 400 vrs W of SE corner) vs [106] (beginning 500 vrs W) confirm these are different parcels on the Boyd League.

operations.description: Replace index-only [120] entry with full PDF-confirmed entry in Phase 1 Supplement
operations.find:
#### Deed [120] — J. H. Bounds → J. E. Bounds (1877-02-26, Limestone)
- **Transaction type**: Gift deed — John Boyd League, 158 acres
- **Citation**: LC Vol. F, Pg. 584, Inst# DR-0000F-00584
- **Property**: 158 acres, John Boyd League
- **Significance**: Earliest documented JH Bounds Boyd Survey disposition — nine years before the Haley acquisition ([106], 1886). Confirms JH Bounds held substantial Boyd Survey land from an undocumented earlier source. J.E. Bounds appears in the 1906 deed [111] grantor list — still living in 1906 but 29 years after this gift.
- **Disposition status**: **DISPOSED OUT OF DIRECT CHAIN** (gift to son). Downstream disposition of J.E. Bounds' 158 acres not yet documented. Pull PDF and research J.E. Bounds chain to close out.
- **Action needed**: Pull LC Vol. F Pg. 584.
operations.replace_with:
#### Deed [120] — J. H. Bounds → J. E. Bounds (1874-10-07, Limestone) — **PDF CONFIRMED**
- **Transaction type**: Warranty/gift deed — surface + minerals, John Boyd League, 158 acres
- **Citation**: LC Book F, Pg. 584–585, Inst# DR-0000F-00584
- **Property**: 158 acres, part of the John Boyd League, Limestone County. Beginning at 400 varas west of the SE corner of said Jno Boyd League, a stake in the south line of said League; thence North with J. V. Bounds west line to Tehuacana Creek about 1900 varas; thence up said creek with its meanderings to the NE corner of a 150-acre survey sold by Faster to Shown and thence by Shown to Alkisson & Faster; thence South 1664 varas to a stake in the south line of said Jno Boyd League and the SE corner of the above referred to 150-acre survey; thence East with Jno Boyd's South League line 500 varas to the place of beginning.
- **Consideration**: $1.00 cash (gift deed)
- **Parties**: Grantor: J. H. Bounds (Limestone County). Grantee: J. E. Bounds (Limestone County). No wife joinder by M. E. Bounds.
- **Acknowledgment**: October 2, 1877, before S. D. Walker, Clerk of the County Court, Limestone County, at Groesbeck. ⚠️ **DATE ANOMALY**: Acknowledgment date (Oct 2, 1877) POSTDATES both the filing date (Feb 26, 1877) and recording date (Mar 1, 1877). Possible explanations: (a) deed filed and recorded prior to formal acknowledgment under early Texas recording practice; (b) scrivener's error in acknowledgment date ("Octy" may be miswriting of "Febry"); (c) the recording cert refers to the original filing while the acknowledgment was added to the certified copy later. HUMAN VERIFICATION REQUIRED against original instrument.
- **Recording history**: Filed February 26, 1877 at 9:30 AM; recorded March 1, 1877 at 3:00 PM; S. D. Walker, C.C.L.C. Texas. Same clerk as deeds [101] (1881) and [109] (1890).
- **Significance**: (a) **Execution date October 7, 1874** — 12 years before the Haley acquisition ([106], 1886), not 9 years as previously estimated from the 1877 filing date. Earliest documented JH Bounds Boyd Survey disposition. (b) **Metes-and-bounds comparison with [106]**: Deed [120] begins 400 vrs W of the SE corner of Boyd League; deed [106] begins 500 vrs W. The [120] south boundary runs 500 vrs west from POB (spanning 400 to 900 vrs W of SE corner). The [106] POB at 500 vrs falls within [120]'s south boundary span — either the parcels share a boundary at 500 vrs, or there is a partial overlap requiring resolution from the [106] full metes-and-bounds. (c) **J. V. Bounds boundary reference**: Deed [120]'s east boundary follows "J. V. Bounds west line," confirming J.V. Bounds held the 62-acre parcel (deed [121]) immediately to the east at the time of execution. This strongly implies deed [121] was also executed in 1874 (not the 1877 filing date). (d) **Neighbor reference**: The 150-acre survey "sold by Faster to Shown and thee by Shown to Alkisson & Faster" is an unidentified neighboring parcel to the northwest; the creek meanders define the north boundary. (e) **Boyd Portfolio impact**: With [120] (158 ac) and [121] (62 ac) as 1874 dispositions, total documented Boyd Survey dispositions = ~500.8 ac, versus 156 ac documented acquisitions ([106] only). JH Bounds' pre-1874 Boyd Survey holding was ≥220 ac from an unlocated source.
- **Disposition status**: **DISPOSED OUT OF DIRECT CHAIN** (gift to son). Downstream disposition of J.E. Bounds' 158 acres not yet documented — see ISSUE-41. J.E. Bounds appears in 1906 deed [111] grantor list (still living 32 years after this gift).
- **Action completed**: PDF pulled; ISSUE-40 item [120] marked PULLED. ISSUE-41 partially advanced (metes-and-bounds confirmed; [121] PDF still needed).
